dir=\"auto\"\u003eCentralized network visibility and continuous asset discovery for homelabs, IT teams, MSPs, and distributed environments.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMonitor devices, detect change, and maintain visibility across remote sites, VLANs, branch offices, and segmented networks from a single interface.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNetAlertX provides a centralized \"Source of Truth\" (NSoT) for network infrastructure. Maintain a real-time inventory of connected devices, identify Shadow IT and unauthorized hardware, support compliance initiatives, and automate operational workflows across distributed customer environments.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDesigned to bridge the gap between simple network scanners and complex SIEM platforms, NetAlertX delivers actionable network intelligence and centralized monitoring without the operational overhead.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" isolated networks, and remote sites.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eUsing \u003ca href=\"https://docs.netalertx.com/ADVISORY_MULTI_SITE_MONITORING\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eSync Nodes\u003c/a\u003e, distributed collectors securely send device inventory and network visibility data back to a central hub, enabling unified monitoring, alerting, and asset tracking across all locations.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis provides MSPs and NOCs with a single operational view of many independent networks, without requiring direct access or centralized scanning infrastructure.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCommon deployments include MSP wallboards, NOC dashboards, multi-site inventory monitoring, and remote office discovery.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFAQ\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-faq\" container has proper network access (e.g., use \u003ccode\u003e--network host\u003c/code\u003e on Linux). Also check that your scan method is properly configured in the UI.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: What is the recommended deployment for high-availability?\u003c/strong\u003e\nA: We recommend deploying via Docker with persistent volume mounts for database integrity and running behind a reverse proxy for secure access.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Will this send any data to the internet?\u003c/strong\u003e\nA: No. All scans and data remain local, unless you set up cloud-based notifications.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Can I use this without Docker?\u003c/strong\u003e\nA: You can install the application directly on your own hardware by following the \u003ca href=\"https://docs.netalertx.com/HW_INSTALL\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ebare metal installation guide\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Where is the data stored?\u003c/strong\u003e\nA: In the \u003ccode\u003e/data/config\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003e/data/db\u003c/code\u003e folders. Back up these folders regularly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTroubleshooting Tips\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-troubleshooting-tips\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Troubleshooting Tips\" href=\"#troubleshooting-tips\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSome scanners (e.g. ARP) may not detect devices on different subnets. See the \u003ca href=\"https://docs.netalertx.com/REMOTE_NETWORKS\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRemote networks guide\u003c/a\u003e for workarounds.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWi-Fi-only networks may require alternate scanners for accurate detection.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNotification throttling may be needed for large networks to prevent spam.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOn some systems, elevated permissions (like \u003ccode\u003eCAP_NET_RAW\u003c/code\u003e) may be needed for low-level scanning.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCheck the \u003ca to install-specific requirements files

The fritzconnection dependency was added to the top-level requirements.txt
when the Fritz!Box plugin was introduced, but the install-specific files
for Proxmox and Ubuntu 24 were not updated. Without the entry in these
files, fresh installations via the install scripts would not install the
dependency.

Requested by reviewer jokob-sk in PR #1592.

Changes:
- Add fritzconnection>=1.15.1 to install/proxmox/requirements.txt
- Add fritzconnection>=1.15.1 to install/ubuntu24/requirements.txt

All three requirements files now declare the fritzconnection dependency
consistently." class="Link--secondary" href="/netalertx/NetAlertX/commit/bc66575f913a32f657883f774e456ac6c9d959e4">Add fritzconnection to install-specific requirements data-pjax="true" title="feat(server): optional default for get_setting_value

get_setting_value returns an empty string when a key is not found,
which forces every call site to remember to treat '' as a sentinel
and provide its own fallback. The fallback is sometimes a hard-coded
default and sometimes a different code path entirely, leading to
inconsistent handling across the codebase.

Add an optional  argument that defaults to '' (preserves
the existing behaviour for every call site) and is returned when
the key is not present. New call sites can opt into a more
meaningful default without changing the function's signature for
existing callers.

Refs #1626." class="Link--secondary" href="/netalertx/NetAlertX/commit/6819f144488874c2aaa6270a6d4f301208c88d93">feat(server): optional default for get_setting_value</a></div></div></td><td><div data-discover="true">requirements.txt</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="Add Fritz!Box device scanner plugin via TR-064 protocol

NetAlertX had no native support for discovering devices connected to
Fritz!Box routers. Users relying on Fritz!Box as their primary home
router had to use generic network scanning (ARP/ICMP), missing
Fritz!Box-specific details like interface type (WiFi/LAN) and
connection status per device.

Changes:
- Add plugin implementation (front/plugins/fritzbox/fritzbox.py)
  Queries all hosts via FritzHosts TR-064 service, normalizes MACs,
  maps interface types (802.11→WiFi, Ethernet→LAN), and writes results
  to CurrentScan via Plugin_Objects. Supports filtering to active-only
  devices and optional guest WiFi monitoring via a synthetic AP device
  with a deterministic locally-administered MAC (02:xx derived from
  Fritz!Box MAC via MD5).

- Add plugin configuration (front/plugins/fritzbox/config.json)
  Defines plugin_type "device_scanner" with settings for host, port,
  credentials, guest WiFi reporting, and active-only filtering.
  Maps scan columns to CurrentScan fields (scanMac, scanLastIP, scanName,
  scanType). Default schedule: every 5 minutes.

- Add plugin documentation (front/plugins/fritzbox/README.md)
  Covers TR-064 protocol basics, quick setup guide, all settings with
  defaults, troubleshooting for common issues (connection refused, auth
  failures, no devices found), and technical details.

- Add fritzconnection>=1.15.1 dependency (requirements.txt)
  Required Python library for TR-064 communication with Fritz!Box.

- Add test suite (test/plugins/test_fritzbox.py:1-298)
  298 lines covering get_connected_devices (active filtering, MAC
  normalization, interface mapping, error resilience), check_guest_wifi_status
  (service detection, SSID-based guest detection, fallback behavior), and
  create_guest_wifi_device (deterministic MAC generation, locally-administered
  bit, fallback MAC, regression anchor with precomputed hash).

Users can now scan Fritz!Box-connected devices natively, seeing per-device
connection status and interface type directly in NetAlertX. Guest WiFi
monitoring provides visibility into guest network state. The plugin
defaults to HTTPS on port 49443 with active-only filtering enabled." class="Link--secondary" href="/netalertx/NetAlertX/commit/5839853f69f561d172ef2c4bb7de53f813a2e11e">Add Fritz!Box device scanner plugin via TR-064 protocol</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time tense="past" datetime="2026-04-06T07:34:14.000Z" title="Apr 6, 2026, 7:34 AM UTC">Apr 6, <p dir="auto">Centralized network visibility and continuous asset discovery for homelabs, IT teams, MSPs, and distributed environments.</p>
<p dir="auto">Monitor devices, detect change, and maintain visibility across remote sites, VLANs, branch offices, and segmented networks from a single interface.</p>
<p dir="auto">NetAlertX provides a centralized "Source of Truth" (NSoT) for network infrastructure. Maintain a real-time inventory of connected devices, identify Shadow IT and unauthorized hardware, support compliance initiatives, and automate operational workflows across distributed customer environments.</p>
<p dir="auto">Designed to bridge the gap between simple network scanners and complex SIEM platforms, NetAlertX delivers actionable network intelligence and centralized monitoring without the operational overhead.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Features</h2><a id="user-content-features" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Features" href="#features"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Discovery & Asset Intelligence</h3><a id="user-content-discovery--asset-intelligence" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Discovery & Asset Intelligence" href="#discovery--asset-intelligence"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Continuous monitoring for unauthorized asset discovery, connection state changes, and IP address management (IPAM) drift. Discovery & scan methods include: <strong>arp-scan</strong>,  <strong>Pi-hole - DB import</strong>,  <strong>Pi-hole - DHCP leases import</strong>, <strong>Generic DHCP leases import</strong>, <strong>UNIFI controller import</strong>, <strong>SNMP-enabled router import</strong>. Check the <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/PLUGINS#readme" rel="nofollow">Plugins</a> docs for a full list of avaliable plugins.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Notification gateways</h3><a id="user-content-notification-gateways" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Notification gateways" href="#notification-gateways"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Send notifications to more than 80+ services, including Telegram via <a href="https://hub.docker.com/r/caronc/apprise" rel="nofollow">Apprise</a>, or use native <a href="https://www.pushsafer.com/" rel="nofollow">Pushsafer</a>, <a href="https://www.pushover.net/" rel="nofollow">Pushover</a>, or <a href="https://ntfy.sh/" rel="nofollow">NTFY</a> publishers.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Integrations and Plugins</h3><a id="user-content-integrations-and-plugins" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Integrations and Plugins" href="#integrations-and-plugins"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Feed your data and device changes into <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/HOME_ASSISTANT" rel="nofollow">Home Assistant</a>, read <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/API" rel="nofollow">API endpoints</a>, or use <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/WEBHOOK_N8N" rel="nofollow">Webhooks</a> to setup custom automation flows. You can also
build your own scanners with the <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/PLUGINS#readme" rel="nofollow">Plugin system</a> in as little as <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cdbxlwiWhv8" rel="nofollow">15 minutes</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Workflows</h3><a id="user-content-workflows" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Workflows" href="#workflows"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/WORKFLOWS" rel="nofollow">workflows module</a> automates IT governance by enforcing device categorization and cleanup policies. Whether you need to assign newly discovered devices to a specific Network Node, auto-group devices from a given vendor, unarchive a device if detected online, or automatically delete devices, this module provides the flexibility to tailor the automations to your needs.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">MSP & Multi-Site Monitoring</h3><a id="user-content-msp--multi-site-monitoring" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: MSP & Multi-Site Monitoring" href="#msp--multi-site-monitoring"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">NetAlertX enables centralized monitoring across remote sites and isolated environments through Sync Nodes for VLANs and branch offices, providing unified visibility of assets across multiple networks. It supports <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/ADVISORY_EYES_ON_GLASS/" rel="nofollow">NOC-style wallboard dashboards</a>, <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/API_METRICS/" rel="nofollow">Prometheus metrics export</a>, workflow automation for device governance, and distributed discovery with centralized alerting for scalable network operations.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Security & Privacy</h2><a id="user-content-security--privacy" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Security & Privacy" href="#security--privacy"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">NetAlertX scans your local network and can store metadata about connected devices. By default, all data is stored <strong>locally</strong>. No information is sent to external services unless you explicitly configure notifications or integrations.</p>
<p dir="auto">Compliance & Hardening:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Run it behind a reverse proxy with authentication</li>
<li>Use firewalls to restrict access to the web UI</li>
<li>Regularly update to the latest version for security patches</li>
<li>Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) via Reverse Proxy: Integrate with your existing SSO/Identity provider for secure dashboard access.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">See <a href="https://github.com/netalertx/NetAlertX/security">Security Best Practices</a> for more details.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Designed for MSPs, NOCs & Distributed Networks</h2><a id="user-content-designed-for-msps-nocs--distributed-networks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Designed for MSPs, NOCs & Distributed Networks" href="#designed-for-msps-nocs--distributed-networks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">NetAlertX supports centralized monitoring across VLANs, branch offices, customer environments, isolated networks, and remote sites.</p>
<p dir="auto">Using <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/ADVISORY_MULTI_SITE_MONITORING" rel="nofollow">Sync Nodes</a>, distributed collectors securely send device inventory and network visibility data back to a central hub, enabling unified monitoring, alerting, and asset tracking across all locations.</p>
<p dir="auto">This provides MSPs and NOCs with a single operational view of many independent networks, without requiring direct access or centralized scanning infrastructure.</p>
<p dir="auto">Common deployments include MSP wallboards, NOC dashboards, multi-site inventory monitoring, and remote office discovery.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">FAQ</h2><a id="user-content-faq" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: FAQ" href="#faq"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Q: How do I monitor VLANs or remote subnets?</strong>
A: Ensure the container has proper network access (e.g., use <code>--network host</code> on Linux). Also check that your scan method is properly configured in the UI.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Q: What is the recommended deployment for high-availability?</strong>
A: We recommend deploying via Docker with persistent volume mounts for database integrity and running behind a reverse proxy for secure access.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Q: Will this send any data to the internet?</strong>
A: No. All scans and data remain local, unless you set up cloud-based notifications.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Q: Can I use this without Docker?</strong>
A: You can install the application directly on your own hardware by following the <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/HW_INSTALL" rel="nofollow">bare metal installation guide</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Q: Where is the data stored?</strong>
A: In the <code>/data/config</code> and <code>/data/db</code> folders. Back up these folders regularly.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Troubleshooting Tips</h2><a id="user-content-troubleshooting-tips" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Troubleshooting Tips" href="#troubleshooting-tips"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Some scanners (e.g. ARP) may not detect devices on different subnets. See the <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/REMOTE_NETWORKS" rel="nofollow">Remote networks guide</a> for workarounds.</li>
<li>Wi-Fi-only networks may require alternate scanners for accurate detection.</li>
<li>Notification throttling may be needed for large networks to prevent spam.</li>
<li>On some systems, elevated permissions (like <code>CAP_NET_RAW</code>) may be needed for low-level scanning.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Check the <a href="https://github.com/netalertx/NetAlertX/issues">GitHub Issues</a> for the latest bug reports and solutions and consult <a href="https://docs.netalertx.com/" rel="nofollow">the official documentation</a>.</p>
